skill.name: creating-financial-models
skill.description: Build DCF valuations, sensitivity tables, scenario analyses, and financial summaries for investment decisions.
skill.summary: Create structured financial models for valuation, sensitivity testing, and scenario planning.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and dismissed as false positives. The flagged Markdown backticks identify script filenames, the weak-crypto alert is the DCF acronym, and the reconnaissance alerts are arithmetic in a binary search routine.

low_findings.title: False Positive: DCF Acronym Matched Weak Crypto Pattern
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptographic algorithm alerts point to SKILL.md line 3, which describes DCF analysis. No cryptographic function or hashing implementation is present at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata text for discounted cash flow analysis. The duplicate static alerts are caused by the DCF acronym, not cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Around Filenames
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to Markdown list items naming dcf_model.py and sensitivity_analysis.py. These are documentation references and are not shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting around filenames. There is no command string, subprocess call, or executable shell context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Binary Search Arithmetic Flagged As Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts point to midpoint and bound updates inside breakeven_analysis. The code performs numeric binary search and does not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines only compute and return numeric values. No OS, environment, filesystem, shell, or network APIs are used there.

content.value_statement: Financial modeling requires consistent assumptions, valuation logic, and sensitivity testing. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured DCF models and scenario analyses.
actual_capabilities: Builds DCF models with projected free cash flows and terminal value calculations.
Calculates WACC from risk-free rate, beta, market premium, debt cost, and capital structure.
Creates one-way and two-way sensitivity analyses for valuation drivers.
Ranks key assumptions with tornado analysis outputs.
Compares best, base, and worst case scenarios with probability-weighted results.
Generates concise valuation summaries from completed model results.
limitations: It depends on user-provided assumptions and financial data quality.
It does not retrieve live market data or company filings.
It is not a substitute for professional investment, tax, or accounting advice.
It does not create Excel files directly from the included scripts.

use_cases.title: Investment Analyst Valuation
use_cases.description: Create a DCF model and test valuation sensitivity to WACC, growth, and margin assumptions.

use_cases.title: Founder Fundraising Model
use_cases.description: Translate operating assumptions into projected cash flows and valuation ranges for investor discussions.

use_cases.title: Corporate Strategy Scenario Review
use_cases.description: Compare strategic scenarios with probability weights and expected valuation outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic DCF
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a five-year DCF model for this company using the attached revenue, EBITDA, capex, working capital, and tax assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Test Key Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Run sensitivity analysis on the DCF model for WACC, terminal growth, EBITDA margin, and revenue growth. Summarize the largest value drivers.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Scenarios
prompt_templates.prompt: Create best, base, and worst case scenarios with probability weights. Show enterprise value, equity value, and expected value for each case.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Valuation Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this financial model for assumption consistency, valuation logic, sensitivity coverage, and decision risks. Recommend specific improvements.

output_examples.input: Build a DCF model for a software company with five years of projections.
output_examples.output: A valuation summary with projected revenue, EBITDA, free cash flow, WACC, terminal value, enterprise value, equity value, and value per share.

output_examples.input: Show how valuation changes if WACC and terminal growth vary.
output_examples.output: A sensitivity table and written summary identifying which assumption changes have the largest valuation impact.

output_examples.input: Compare expansion scenarios with different margin and growth assumptions.
output_examples.output: A scenario comparison with probabilities, valuation outcomes, weighted expected value, and key risks for each case.

best_practices: Provide clean historical financials before asking for valuation outputs.
Document every major assumption and its source.
Use sensitivity and scenario analysis before relying on a single valuation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat default assumptions as market evidence.
Do not use the model output as investment advice without expert review.
Do not compare scenarios unless their assumptions are clearly defined.

faq.question: What financial models can this skill help create?
faq.answer: It supports DCF valuation, WACC calculation, sensitivity analysis, tornado analysis, and scenario comparison.

faq.question: Does it include live market data?
faq.answer: No. You must provide market inputs, company data, and assumptions.

faq.question: Can it support M&A or project finance work?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance describes M&A, project finance, LBO, and corporate valuation use cases.

faq.question: Does it replace a financial advisor?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure analysis, but professional judgment is required.

faq.question: What tools are included?
faq.answer: The skill includes Python modules for DCF valuation and sensitivity analysis.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
